_comment: Hand-authored descriptions for the bundled ELM11 C API. Keyed by function name (assumed unique across headers). Merged into docs_c_data.json by scripts/build_c_docs.py.

functions.atof_float: Parse a numeric string into a float (libc-style atof, no errno reporting).
functions.isalnum: Return non-zero if `c` is alphanumeric (libc ctype).
functions.isalpha: Return non-zero if `c` is alphabetic (libc ctype).
functions.isascii: Return non-zero if `c` is a 7-bit ASCII character.
functions.isblank: Return non-zero if `c` is a blank character (space or tab).
functions.iscntrl: Return non-zero if `c` is a control character.
functions.isdigit: Return non-zero if `c` is a decimal digit.
functions.isgraph: Return non-zero if `c` is a printable non-space character.
functions.islower: Return non-zero if `c` is a lower-case letter.
functions.isprint: Return non-zero if `c` is printable, including space.
functions.ispunct: Return non-zero if `c` is a punctuation character.
functions.isupper: Return non-zero if `c` is an upper-case letter.
functions.isxdigit: Return non-zero if `c` is a hexadecimal digit.
functions.tolower: Convert `c` to lower-case if it's an upper-case letter; otherwise return unchanged.
functions.toupper: Convert `c` to upper-case if it's a lower-case letter; otherwise return unchanged.
functions.spi_flashio: Low-level SPI flash transfer — exchanges `length` bytes through `pdata`; `wren` selects write-enable.
functions.spi_flashio_wrapper: Build-switched wrapper around `spi_flashio` so call sites don't need to know which transfer implementation is active.
functions.init_flash: Initialise the on-board SPI flash interface and read its identification data.
functions.read_board_id: Read the unique board identifier from flash into `*board_id`.
functions.read_flash_page_byte: Read a single byte at `page_addr` from the flash into `*page_byte`.
functions.read_flash_page: Read a full flash page at `page_addr` into the caller-supplied `page_data` buffer.
functions.write_flash_page: Write a full flash page at `page_addr` from `page_data` (caller is responsible for prior erase if required).
functions.write_io_type_to_flash: Persist the I/O-type setting for `io_index` (`io_config`) to non-volatile flash.
functions.read_io_type_from_flash: Return the persisted I/O type stored in flash for `io_index`.
functions.reset_program_name_start_on_boot: Clear the start-on-boot program slot so no program runs automatically at reset.
functions.set_program_name_start_on_boot: Mark `prog_name` as the program to launch automatically on next boot.
functions.get_program_name_start_on_boot: Return a pointer to the configured start-on-boot program name (NULL/empty if none).
functions.is_program_start_on_boot_valid: Return true if a valid start-on-boot program is currently configured.
functions.clear_storage_metadata_slot_for_addr: Free the storage-metadata slot at `prog_addr`, marking it available for reuse.
functions.set_storage_metadata_slot_for_addr: Mark the storage-metadata slot at `prog_addr` as occupied.
functions.set_prompt_formats_start_on_boot: Persist the time and CPU prompt formats to use at boot.
functions.get_time_prompt_format_start_on_boot: Return the persisted boot-time time-prompt format.
functions.get_cpu_prompt_format_start_on_boot: Return the persisted boot-time CPU-prompt format.
functions.make_new_program_entry: Allocate a fresh storage slot for `prog_name` and return its starting flash address via `*prog_addr`.
functions.get_start_addr_of_program: Look up the flash starting address for an existing program named `prog_name`.
functions.read_program_pages: Read up to `num_pages` flash pages starting at `page_start_addr` into `buffer`; sets `*last_page` when EOF reached.
functions.upload_program: Receive a Lua program over the serial link and write it into flash under `prog_name`.
functions.delete_program: Remove `prog_name` from flash, freeing its storage slots.
functions.delete_all_programs: Erase every stored program from flash; `print_lvl` controls progress output verbosity.
functions.print_all_program_names: Print the list of stored program names to the active output stream.
functions.print_all_program_addrs: Print every stored program along with its flash address.
functions.print_num_saved_programs: Print the count of programs currently stored in flash.
functions.print_program_data_slot_utilisation: Print a table showing how many flash data slots are in use vs. available.
functions.user_exit_program_interrupt: Interrupt-context flag check: returns non-zero if the user has requested the running program to exit ('q'+enter).
functions.get_cpu_addr_access_low_water_mark: Return the lowest CPU memory address recently accessed (used for memory-usage tracing).
functions.get_cpu_addr_access_high_water_mark: Return the highest CPU memory address recently accessed.
functions.print_cpu_access_trace: Print the captured CPU memory-access trace to the output stream.
functions.init_globals: Initialise core global state — IDs, timers, I/O config — at boot.
functions.read_cpu_freq: Read and cache the CPU clock frequency from the FPGA fabric.
functions.read_core_info: Read and cache per-core identification data.
functions.get_num_cores: Return the total number of CPU cores configured in the build.
functions.get_core_id: Return the ID of the currently executing core (0-based).
functions.get_cpu_freq_hz: Return the cached CPU frequency in Hz.
functions.get_cpu_freq_khz: Return the cached CPU frequency in kHz.
functions.get_cpu_freq_mhz: Return the cached CPU frequency in MHz.
functions.was_reboot_due_to_stack_overflow: Return non-zero if the most recent reboot was triggered by a stack-overflow trap.
functions.read_uart_cfg: Read and cache the user-comms UART configuration from the FPGA.
functions.uart_init: Initialise the user-comms UART (baud, parity, etc.) using the cached config.
functions.user_comms_enable_rx_interrupt_mode: Switch the user-comms UART receiver to interrupt-driven mode.
functions.user_comms_disable_rx_interrupt_mode: Switch the user-comms UART receiver back to polling mode.
functions.uart_read_char: Non-blocking poll: return the next character from the UART, or 0 if none waiting.
functions.uart_read_char_blocking: Block until a character arrives on the UART and return it.
functions.uart_read_char_maxwait_ms: Block for at most `ms` milliseconds waiting for a UART character; 0 on timeout.
functions.uart_write_char: Send a single character `c` over the user-comms UART.
functions.uart_write_string: Send the null-terminated string `p` over the user-comms UART.
functions.uart_write_hexidecimal: Print `v` as a `digits`-wide hexadecimal value over the UART.
functions.uart_write_ptr_hexidecimal: Print the address held by `v` as a `digits`-wide hex value.
functions.uart_write_uint32_t: Print `v` as an unsigned 32-bit decimal over the UART.
functions.uart_write_float: Print floating-point value `f` over the UART.
functions.uart_write_string_with_prompts: Print `str` to the UART, prefixed by the configured CPU/time prompts; honours command-mode formatting.
functions.delay_milliseconds: Busy-wait for `milliseconds` (uses the general timer; not interruptible).
functions.get_uart_cfg: Return the cached user-comms UART configuration register value.
functions.get_xbar_lock_cfg: Return the configured cross-bar lock-channel mask for the current core.
functions.get_xbar_lock_tx_fifo_depth: Return the lock-channel TX FIFO depth between this core and `core_id`.
functions.get_xbar_unlock_cfg: Return the configured cross-bar unlock-channel mask for the current core.
functions.get_xbar_lock_rx_fifo_depth: Return the lock-channel RX FIFO depth between this core and `core_id`.
functions.get_xbar_data_tx_cfg: Return the configured cross-bar data-TX channel mask for the current core.
functions.get_xbar_data_tx_fifo_depth: Return the data-TX FIFO depth from this core to `core_id`.
functions.get_xbar_data_rx_cfg: Return the configured cross-bar data-RX channel mask for the current core.
functions.get_xbar_data_rx_fifo_depth: Return the data-RX FIFO depth from `core_id` to this core.
functions.xbar_lock: Block until the cross-bar lock barrier with `core` is unlocked, then re-lock it.
functions.xbar_lock_nonblocking: Attempt to lock the cross-bar barrier with `core`; sets `*success` to indicate result.
functions.xbar_unlock: Block until the cross-bar lock barrier with `core` is locked, then unlock it.
functions.xbar_unlock_nonblocking: Attempt to unlock the cross-bar barrier with `core`; sets `*success` to indicate result.
functions.xbar_data_tx: Block until the cross-bar data channel to `core` is ready, then send `byte`.
functions.xbar_data_tx_nonblocking: Attempt to send `byte` over the cross-bar data channel to `core`; sets `*success` to indicate result.
functions.xbar_data_rx: Block until a byte arrives from `core` over the cross-bar; stores it in `*byte`.
functions.xbar_data_rx_nonblocking: Attempt to receive a byte from `core` over the cross-bar; sets `*success` if data was available.
functions.init_cpu_prompt_format_for_repl: Reset the CPU prompt format to the REPL default at session start.
functions.increment_cpu_prompt_format: Cycle the CPU prompt to the next configured format.
functions.get_time_prompt_format: Return the currently active time-prompt format.
functions.get_cpu_prompt_format: Return the currently active CPU-prompt format.
functions.set_time_prompt_format: Set the active time-prompt format to `pf`.
functions.set_cpu_prompt_format: Set the active CPU-prompt format to `cpu_prompt_format`.
functions.increment_time_prompt_format: Cycle the time prompt to the next configured format.
functions.is_valid_time_prompt_format: Return non-zero if `pf` is one of the recognised time-prompt formats.
functions.time_prompt_format_to_string: Return a human-readable name for time-prompt format `pf`.
functions.cpu_prompt_format_to_string: Return a human-readable name for CPU-prompt format `pf`.
functions.read_performance_timer_cfg: Read and cache the performance-timer presence/config from the FPGA.
functions.get_performance_timer_cfg: Return the cached performance-timer configuration value.
functions.clear_system_time_caches: Invalidate cached system-time values so the next read fetches fresh data.
functions.trigger_caching_of_system_time: Capture the current system time into the named cache slot.
functions.read_general_timer_cfg: Read and cache the general-timer presence/config from the FPGA.
functions.get_general_timer_cfg: Return the cached general-timer configuration value.
functions.cancel_interruptible_sleep: Wake any in-progress interruptible sleep on this core.
functions.doing_interruptible_sleep: Return non-zero if the current core is presently inside an interruptible sleep.
functions.sleep_milliseconds: Sleep for `ms` milliseconds; honours `interruptible_options` to allow user-interrupt cancellation.
functions.continue_sleep_milliseconds: Resume an interrupted millisecond sleep with the same options.
functions.continue_sleep_microseconds: Resume an interrupted microsecond sleep with the same options.
functions.set_board_leds_state: Apply a vector of LED states (`led_st[]`, length `num_leds`) to the on-board LEDs.
functions.set_board_led_state: Set a single on-board LED `board_led` to state `led_st`.
functions.get_board_led_state: Return the current state of on-board LED `board_led`.
functions.toggle_board_led_state: Invert the state of on-board LED `board_led`.
functions.set_all_board_leds_off: Force every on-board LED to the off state.
functions.set_board_leds_hw_mode: Hand on-board LED control back to the FPGA hardware (no longer driven by software).
functions.set_board_leds_sw_mode: Take software control of the on-board LEDs (overrides hardware-driven patterns).
functions.set_software_interrupt_enabled_state: Enable or disable the global software-interrupt master switch.
functions.software_interrupts_enabled: Return non-zero if global software interrupts are currently enabled.
functions.set_repl_interrupt_mode: Enable or disable REPL-interrupt mode for the current session.
functions.repl_interrupt_mode_enabled: Return non-zero if REPL interrupts are currently active.
functions.remove_trailing_nl_and_cr: Strip any trailing CR/LF characters from `str` in place.
functions.set_successfully_booted_hardware_flag: Mark the boot sequence as having reached a known-good state.
functions.get_successfully_booted_hardware_flag: Return non-zero if the boot sequence completed successfully.
functions.valid_hardware_id: Return non-zero if the cached hardware ID matches an expected value.
functions.reboot_board: Trigger a hardware reboot of the board.
functions.get_hardware_watchdog_timer_ms: Return the current watchdog timer countdown value in milliseconds.
functions.get_hardware_watchdog_timeout_ms: Return the configured watchdog timeout value in milliseconds.
functions.reset_hardware_watchdog_ms: Pet the hardware watchdog (resets its countdown to the configured timeout).
functions.get_memory_trace_enabled: Return non-zero if memory access tracing is currently enabled.
functions.read_fpga_io_bus_enabled: Return non-zero if the FPGA fabric I/O bus is enabled in this build.
functions.get_stack_memory_total_kb: Return the total stack memory size in kilobytes.
functions.get_stack_low_water_mark: Return the lowest free-stack value observed since the last reset.
functions.reset_stack_low_water_mark: Reset the stack low-water-mark tracking to current free-stack size.
functions.reset_stack_overflow_occurred_flag: Clear the latched 'stack overflow occurred' flag.
functions.set_out_of_heap_memory_flag: Latch a flag indicating heap exhaustion was encountered (called by allocators).
functions.get_heap_memory_total_kb: Return total configured heap size in kilobytes.
functions.get_heap_memory_free: Return the current number of free bytes in the heap.
functions.get_heap_memory_free_low_water_mark: Return the smallest free-heap value observed since the last reset.
functions.reset_heap_low_water_mark: Reset the heap low-water-mark tracking to current free-heap size.
functions.fgets_with_history: Read a line into `buffer` (max `size` bytes) with up/down arrow history support; updates the history buffer.
functions.get_cursor_position: Query the terminal for cursor position via ANSI escape; returns it via `*row` and `*col`.
functions.get_console_rows_with_default_on_failure: Return the terminal row count, falling back to a built-in default on query failure.
functions.get_console_cols_with_default_on_failure: Return the terminal column count, falling back to a built-in default on query failure.
functions.clear_console_and_history_and_reset_curs: Clear screen + scrollback and move cursor to origin via ANSI escapes.
functions.move_cursor_to_top_of_screen: Move the terminal cursor to row 1, column 1.
functions.move_cursor_to_bottom_of_screen: Move the terminal cursor to the bottom row.
functions.print_full_page_print_footer: Render a paginated 'page X of Y' footer for long output listings.
functions.disable_user_comms_rx_esc_seq_filter: Disable the user-comms RX escape-sequence filter (lets escape codes pass through).
functions.enable_user_comms_rx_esc_seq_filter: Enable the user-comms RX escape-sequence filter (strips ANSI codes from input).
functions.init_io: Initialise all I/O pin state at boot, applying any persisted configuration.
functions.io_get_num_pins: Return the number of physical I/O pins this build supports.
functions.update_io_type: Apply a new I/O type to `io_index` immediately (without persisting to flash).
functions.get_io_type_as_str: Return the active I/O type for `io_index` as a printable string (e.g. "GPIO_OUT").
functions.user_io_num_from_str: Parse a user-facing PIN string (e.g. "PIN3") into its integer pin index.
functions.is_hardware_shared_spi_config: Return non-zero if the hardware uses the shared-SPI configuration variant.
functions.get_smallest_index_dynamic_iotype_offset: Return the lowest enum value reserved for dynamic (shared-SPI) IO types.
functions.get_max_iotypes: Return the highest valid I/O type enum value for this build.
functions.get_io_type: Return the currently configured I/O type for `user_io_num`.
functions.valid_user_io_num: Return non-zero if `user_io_num` is a valid pin index for this build.
functions.valid_user_io_num_as_str: Return non-zero if `user_io_num_str` parses as a valid pin ("PIN<n>").
functions.valid_formatting_for_user_io_num_as_str: Return non-zero if `user_io_num_str` matches the expected PIN<n> format (regardless of range).
functions.valid_io_type_as_str: Return non-zero if `io_type_str` names a recognised I/O type.
functions.valid_standalone_io_type: Return non-zero if `io_type` is a standalone (non-shared-SPI) IO type.
functions.valid_io_baud: Return non-zero if `baud_rate` is one of the supported UART baud rates.
functions.can_set_baud_for_io_type: Return non-zero if `iotype` is a UART variant that accepts a baud setting.
functions.is_baud_config_applicable_to_io: Return non-zero if pin `io_index` is currently configured as UART (so baud applies).
functions.get_baud_config_for_io: Return the active UART baud rate for pin `io_index`.
functions.set_baud_config_for_io: Set the UART baud rate of pin `io_index` to `baud`.
functions.valid_io_pwm_freq: Return non-zero if `pwm_freq_khz` is within the supported PWM frequency range.
functions.get_pwm_clock_divider: Return the FPGA clock divider corresponding to `pwm_freq_khz`.
functions.is_pwm_config_applicable_to_io: Return non-zero if pin `io_index` is currently configured as PWM.
functions.get_pwm_config_for_io: Return the active PWM frequency setting (kHz) for pin `io_index`.
functions.set_pwm_config_for_io: Set the PWM frequency for pin `io_index` to `pwm_freq_khz`.
functions.get_spi_freq_from_clock_divider: Convert an FPGA clock divider value back to its equivalent SPI frequency (kHz).
functions.get_spi_clock_divider: Return the FPGA clock divider corresponding to `spi_freq_khz`.
functions.is_spi_config_applicable_to_io: Return non-zero if pin `io_index` is currently configured for SPI.
functions.get_spi_config_for_io: Return the active SPI frequency setting (kHz) for pin `io_index`.
functions.set_spi_config_for_standalone_spi_io: Set SPI frequency for an independently-clocked SPI pin.
functions.set_spi_config_for_io: Set the SPI frequency for pin `io_index` to `spi_freq_khz`.
functions.valid_io_spi_freq: Return non-zero if `spi_freq_khz` is within the supported SPI frequency range.
functions.configure_spi_out_for_shared_spi_config_: Apply shared-SPI hardware configuration for an output-direction pin.
functions.configure_spi_in_for_shared_spi_config_h: Apply shared-SPI hardware configuration for an input-direction pin.
functions.get_software_interrupt_types_for_io: Return the bitmask of currently-enabled software interrupt types on pin `io_index`.
functions.set_software_interrupt_types_for_io: Replace the enabled software-interrupt-type bitmask on pin `io_index`.
functions.enable_software_interrupt_types_for_io: OR `interrupt_mask` into the enabled software-interrupt-type set on pin `io_index`.
functions.disable_software_interrupt_types_for_io: Clear the bits in `interrupt_mask` from the enabled set on pin `io_index`.
functions.get_software_interrupt_vector: Return the global vector of pending software interrupts (one bit per pin).
functions.get_software_interrupts_for_io: Return the bitmask of currently-pending interrupts on pin `io_index`.
functions.acknowledge_software_interrupts_for_io: Acknowledge (clear) the bits in `interrupts` for pin `io_index`.
functions.set_io_type: Set the I/O type for pin `io_index` to `io_type`.
functions.set_io_type_from_str: Set the I/O type for pin `io_index` from a string name (e.g. "GPIO_OUT").
functions.set_gpio: Drive the GPIO output of `user_io_num` to `gpio_val` (LOW/HIGH/TOGGLE).
functions.get_gpio: Read the current GPIO input level on `user_io_num` into `*gpio_val`.
functions.set_pwm: Set the PWM duty cycle of `user_io_num` to `pwm_val` (0..PWM_VALUE_MAX).
functions.spi_tx_char: Transmit one byte (`tx_char`) over the SPI master on `user_io_num`.
functions.spi_tx_byte: Transmit one byte (`tx_byte`) over the SPI master on `user_io_num`.
functions.spi_tx_int: Transmit a 32-bit word (`tx_word`) over the SPI master on `user_io_num`.
functions.spi_rx_byte: Receive a single byte from the SPI slave on `user_io_num` into `*rx_byte`.
functions.uart_tx_char: Transmit one byte (`tx_char`) over the UART on `user_io_num`.
functions.uart_tx_byte: Transmit one byte (`tx_byte`) over the UART on `user_io_num`.
functions.uart_tx_int: Transmit a 32-bit word (`tx_word`) over the UART on `user_io_num`.
functions.uart_rx_byte: Block-receive one byte from the UART on `user_io_num` into `*uart_rx_val`.
functions.uart_rx_byte_nonblocking: Non-blocking receive: returns the next UART byte if available, with `*uart_rx_val_valid` set accordingly.
functions.standalone_io_type_tostring: Return the string name for a standalone I/O type enum value.
functions.relational_io_type_tostring: Return the string name for a relational (paired) I/O type enum value.
functions.analog_io_type_tostring: Return the string name for an analog I/O type enum value.
functions.reset_io_type_config: Reset pin `io_index` to type NONE (releases any active configuration).
functions.reset_all_io_config: Reset every pin to type NONE.
functions.print_io_type_config: Print the I/O type configuration of every pin.
functions.print_io_baud_config: Print the UART baud configuration for every UART-configured pin.
functions.print_io_pwm_config: Print the PWM frequency for every PWM-configured pin.
functions.print_io_spi_config: Print the SPI frequency for every SPI-configured pin.
functions.print_io_capabilities: Print the per-pin capability matrix (which I/O types each pin supports).
functions.save_io_config_as_start_on_boot: Persist the active I/O configuration to flash for use on next boot.
functions.load_start_on_boot_config_for_all_io: Apply the persisted boot-time I/O configuration to every pin.
functions.get_default_spi_clock_divider: Return the FPGA clock divider used at the default SPI frequency.
functions.valid_analog_io_type_as_str: Return non-zero if `analog_type_str` names a recognised analog I/O type.
functions.valid_analog_io_type: Return non-zero if `analog_type` is a recognised analog I/O type enum.
functions.valid_analog_dac_user_io_num: Return non-zero if `analog_dac_user_io_num` is a valid DAC channel index.
functions.valid_analog_adc_user_io_num: Return non-zero if `analog_adc_user_io_num` is a valid ADC channel index.
functions.valid_user_analog_io_num_as_str: Return non-zero if `user_io_num_str` parses as a valid analog channel.
functions.valid_user_analog_io_num: Return non-zero if `user_io_num` is a valid analog channel index.
functions.initialise_memory: Initialise the heap allocator and memory metadata at boot.
functions.get_memory_total_bytes: Return the total heap size in bytes.
functions.get_memory_free_bytes: Return the current free heap in bytes.
functions.free: Standard free() — release the allocation pointed to by `ptr`.
functions.memcmp: Standard memcmp() — compare `n` bytes between `s1` and `s2`.
functions.get_memory_usage_trace_enabled: Return non-zero if memory-usage tracing is currently enabled.
functions.enable_memory_usage_trace: Begin recording per-allocation memory usage for diagnostics.
functions.reset_memory_free_bytes_min_observed: Reset the lowest-observed free-bytes counter to the current value.
functions.get_memory_free_bytes_min_observed: Return the smallest free-heap size observed since boot/reset.
functions.allow_only_fast_heap_large_regions: Restrict the fast-heap allocator to large regions only.
functions.allow_all_fast_heap_regions: Allow the fast-heap allocator to use any region size.
functions.report_memory_layout: Print a summary of the heap region layout to the output stream.
functions.set_memory_alloc_size_occurances_track_a: Switch the alloc-size occurrence counter to accumulating mode (counts persist).
functions.set_memory_alloc_size_occurances_track_n: Switch the alloc-size occurrence counter to non-accumulating mode.
functions.print_memory_alloc_size_occurances: Print the table of allocation-size occurrence counts.
functions.print_fast_memory_state: Print the current state of the fast-heap allocator (region usage).
functions.print_fast_memory_alloc_size_occurances: Print the fast-heap allocation-size occurrence counts.
functions.reset_fast_memory_alloc_size_occurances: Clear the fast-heap allocation-size occurrence counters.
functions.sprintf_: Compact-printf sprintf — format into `buffer` (no overflow check).
functions.snprintf_: Compact-printf snprintf — format into `buffer` with at most `count` bytes written.
functions.vsnprintf_: Compact-printf vsnprintf — variadic-list version of snprintf.
functions.init_mem_buf: Initialise the standard-library file-buffering memory pool.
functions.system: Pass `command` to the host shell. Stub on this platform; returns -1 if unsupported.
functions.printf: Standard printf — write a formatted string to stdout.
functions.fclose: Close the file `stream` and release its buffers.
functions.fread: Read `nmemb` items of `size` bytes from `stream` into `ptr`.
functions.fwrite: Write `nmemb` items of `size` bytes from `ptr` to `stream`.
functions.fwrite_hex_linewidth8bytes: Write `ptr` to `stream` as a hex dump, eight bytes per line.
functions.fgetc: Read the next character from `stream` (EOF on end-of-file).
functions.fseek: Reposition the file pointer of `stream` to `offset` bytes from `whence`.
functions.ftell: Return the current byte offset of `stream` from its start.
functions.getc: Read the next character from `stream` (typically a macro alias for fgetc).
functions.getchar: Read the next character from stdin.
functions.puts: Write `s` followed by a newline to stdout.
functions.ungetc: Push character `c` back onto `stream` so the next read returns it.
functions.strcmp: Lexicographic compare of null-terminated strings `s1` and `s2`.
functions.strcmp_volatile: Volatile-pointer variant of strcmp; safe against concurrently-modified buffers.
functions.strlen: Return the length (in bytes) of the null-terminated string `s`.
functions.strcoll: Locale-aware compare of `s1` and `s2`. On this platform behaves identically to strcmp.
functions.strspn: Return the length of the prefix of `s` made up of characters in `accept`.
functions.strncmp: Compare up to `n` bytes of `s1` and `s2` lexicographically.
functions.my_clock: Return a free-running clock-tick count (board-specific replacement for clock()).
functions.my_time: Read the current system time into the caller-supplied buffer (board-specific replacement for time()).
functions.strtod_f: Parse a float from `nptr`; if `endptr` is non-NULL, sets it to the first unparsed character.
functions.strtol: Parse a signed long from `nptr` in the given `base` (or auto-detect when base is 0).
